metadata.title:
COMPANY ASKS


INVESTIGATION

Accepts Full Legal Responsibility for Ter


rible Spokane & Inland Wreck.

pages.raw_text:
The Spokane & Inland company
accepts full legal responsibility for
the wreck Saturday, as the guaran-
tor Of the efficiency of its men.


The company will ask that the
moral responsibility be fixed by a
commission composed of prominent
Washington and Idaho attorneys
and newspapermen.


The company has nothing to con-
ceal and will welcome the most
searching investigation that can be
made.


This Is the statement today of
win G. Graves. attorney for the
Spokane & Inland company.


The men already invited to act on
the commission are United States
District Attorney Avery of this
city, Prosecuting Attorney Pugh.
Corporation Counsel Conner, Unit
ed States District Attorney Lngen-
felter of Idaho, Prosecuting Attor-
ney Potts of Kootenai county.


The date of hearing has not yet
been set. Railroad men will prob
ably be added to the commission.


The latest theory to account for
the extra train pulling out OF Occur
d'Alene on the regulars time Is
that after receiving orders Camp
bell ran down into the yards, the
dispatcher supposing that he in
tended to wait there. Instead he
pulled out for Spokane.
